groups.description: Groups is a slice of group definitions containing related MCP servers

last_updated.description: LastUpdated is the timestamp when the registry was last updated, in RFC3339 format

remote_servers.description:
RemoteServers is a map of server names to their corresponding remote server definitions
These are MCP servers accessed via HTTP/HTTPS using the thv proxy command

servers.description: Servers is a map of server names to their corresponding server definitions

version.description: Version is the schema version of the registry

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_cmd_thv-ope.description:
PerUser token bucket configuration for this tool.
+optional

maxTokens.description:
MaxTokens is the maximum number of tokens (bucket capacity).
This is also the burst size: the maximum number of requests that can be served
instantaneously before the bucket is depleted.
+kubebuilder:validation:Required
+kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_cmd_thv-ope.description:
RateLimitConfig contains the CRD rate limiting configuration.
When set, rate limiting middleware is added to the proxy middleware chain.

tools.description:
Tools defines per-tool rate limit overrides.
Each entry applies additional rate limits to calls targeting a specific tool name.
A request must pass both the server-level limit and the per-tool limit.
+listType=map
+listMapKey=name
+optional

name.description:
Name is the MCP tool name this limit applies to.
+kubebuilder:validation:Required
+kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_audit.C.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
AuditConfig contains the audit logging configuration

component.description:
Component is the component name to use in audit events.
+optional

detectApplicationErrors.description:
DetectApplicationErrors controls whether the audit middleware inspects
JSON-RPC response bodies for application-level errors when the HTTP
status code indicates success (2xx). When enabled, a small prefix of
the response body is buffered to detect JSON-RPC error fields,
independent of the IncludeResponseData setting.
+kubebuilder:default=true
+optional

enabled.description:
Enabled controls whether audit logging is enabled.
When true, enables audit logging with the configured options.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

eventTypes.description:
EventTypes specifies which event types to audit. If empty, all events are audited.
+optional

excludeEventTypes.description:
ExcludeEventTypes specifies which event types to exclude from auditing.
This takes precedence over EventTypes.
+optional

includeRequestData.description:
IncludeRequestData determines whether to include request data in audit logs.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

includeResponseData.description:
IncludeResponseData determines whether to include response data in audit logs.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

logFile.description:
LogFile specifies the file path for audit logs. If empty, logs to stdout.
+optional

maxDataSize.description:
MaxDataSize limits the size of request/response data included in audit logs (in bytes).
+kubebuilder:default=1024
+optional

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_auth.To.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
OIDCConfig contains OIDC configuration

allowPrivateIP.description: AllowPrivateIP allows JWKS/OIDC endpoints on private IP addresses

audience.description: Audience is the expected audience for the token

authTokenFile.description: AuthTokenFile is the path to file containing bearer token for authentication

cacertPath.description: CACertPath is the path to the CA certificate bundle for HTTPS requests

clientID.description: ClientID is the OIDC client ID

clientSecret.description: ClientSecret is the optional OIDC client secret for introspection

insecureAllowHTTP.description:
InsecureAllowHTTP allows HTTP (non-HTTPS) OIDC issuers for development/testing
WARNING: This is insecure and should NEVER be used in production

introspectionURL.description: IntrospectionURL is the optional introspection endpoint for validating tokens

issuer.description: Issuer is the OIDC issuer URL (e.g., https://accounts.google.com)

jwksurl.description: JWKSURL is the URL to fetch the JWKS from

resourceURL.description: ResourceURL is the explicit resource URL for OAuth discovery (RFC 9728)

scopes.description:
Scopes is the list of OAuth scopes to advertise in the well-known endpoint (RFC 9728)
If empty, defaults to ["openid"]

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_auth_aw.description: AWSStsConfig contains AWS STS token exchange configuration for accessing AWS services

fallback_role_arn.description: FallbackRoleArn is the IAM role ARN to assume when no role mapping matches.

region.description: Region is the AWS region for STS and SigV4 signing.

role_claim.description: RoleClaim is the JWT claim to use for role mapping (default: "groups").

role_mappings.description: RoleMappings maps JWT claim values to IAM roles with priority.

service.description: Service is the AWS service name for SigV4 signing (default: "aws-mcp").

session_duration.description: SessionDuration is the duration in seconds for assumed role credentials (default: 3600).

session_name_claim.description: SessionNameClaim is the JWT claim to use for role session name (default: "sub").

claim.description:
Claim is the simple claim value to match (e.g., group name).
Internally compiles to a CEL expression: "<claim_value>" in claims["<role_claim>"]
Mutually exclusive with Matcher.

matcher.description:
Matcher is a CEL expression for complex matching against JWT claims.
The expression has access to a "claims" variable containing all JWT claims.
Examples:
  - "admins" in claims["groups"]
  - claims["sub"] == "user123" && !("act" in claims)
Mutually exclusive with Claim.

priority.description:
Priority determines selection order (lower number = higher priority).
When multiple mappings match, the one with the lowest priority is selected.
When nil (omitted), the mapping has the lowest possible priority, and
configuration order acts as tie-breaker via stable sort.

role_arn.description: RoleArn is the IAM role ARN to assume when this mapping matches.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_auth_re.description: RemoteAuthConfig contains OAuth configuration for remote MCP servers

cached_client_id.description:
Cached DCR client credentials for persistence across restarts.
These are obtained during Dynamic Client Registration and needed to refresh tokens.
ClientID is stored as plain text since it's public information.

cached_refresh_token_ref.description:
Cached OAuth token reference for persistence across restarts.
The refresh token is stored securely in the secret manager, and this field
contains the reference to retrieve it (e.g., "OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN_workload").
This enables session restoration without requiring a new browser-based login.

cached_reg_token_ref.description:
RegistrationAccessToken is used to update/delete the client registration.
Stored as a secret reference since it's sensitive.

cached_secret_expiry.description:
ClientSecretExpiresAt indicates when the client secret expires (if provided by the DCR server).
A zero value means the secret does not expire.

resource.description: Resource is the OAuth 2.0 resource indicator (RFC 8707).

scope_param_name.description:
ScopeParamName overrides the query parameter name used to send scopes in the
authorization URL. When empty, the standard "scope" parameter is used.
Some providers require a non-standard name (e.g., Slack uses "user_scope").

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_auth_to.description: TokenExchangeConfig contains token exchange configuration for external authentication

audience.description: Audience is the target audience for the exchanged token

client_id.description: ClientID is the OAuth 2.0 client identifier

client_secret.description: ClientSecret is the OAuth 2.0 client secret

external_token_header_name.description: ExternalTokenHeaderName is the name of the custom header to use when HeaderStrategy is "custom"

header_strategy.description:
HeaderStrategy determines how to inject the token
Valid values: HeaderStrategyReplace (default), HeaderStrategyCustom

scopes.description: Scopes is the list of scopes to request for the exchanged token

subject_token_type.description:
SubjectTokenType specifies the type of the subject token being exchanged.
Common values: tokenTypeAccessToken (default), tokenTypeIDToken, tokenTypeJWT.
If empty, defaults to tokenTypeAccessToken.

token_url.description: TokenURL is the OAuth 2.0 token endpoint URL

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_auth_up.description:
UpstreamSwapConfig contains configuration for upstream token swap middleware.
When set along with EmbeddedAuthServerConfig, this middleware exchanges ToolHive JWTs
for upstream IdP tokens before forwarding requests to the MCP server.

custom_header_name.description: CustomHeaderName is the header name when HeaderStrategy is "custom".

header_strategy.description: HeaderStrategy determines how to inject the token: "replace" (default) or "custom".

provider_name.description:
ProviderName identifies which upstream provider's tokens to retrieve for injection.
This is required and must match a configured upstream provider name.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
OAuth2Config contains OAuth 2.0-specific configuration.
Required when Type is "oauth2", must be nil when Type is "oidc".

additional_authorization_params.description:
AdditionalAuthorizationParams are extra query parameters to include in
authorization requests. Useful for provider-specific parameters like
Google's access_type=offline.

authorization_endpoint.description: AuthorizationEndpoint is the URL for the OAuth authorization endpoint.

client_id.description: ClientID is the OAuth 2.0 client identifier registered with the upstream IDP.

client_secret_env_var.description:
ClientSecretEnvVar is the name of an environment variable containing the client secret.
Mutually exclusive with ClientSecretFile. Optional for public clients using PKCE.

client_secret_file.description:
ClientSecretFile is the path to a file containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret.
Mutually exclusive with ClientSecretEnvVar. Optional for public clients using PKCE.

redirect_uri.description:
RedirectURI is the callback URL where the upstream IDP will redirect after authentication.
When not specified, defaults to `{issuer}/oauth/callback`.

scopes.description: Scopes are the OAuth scopes to request from the upstream IDP.

token_endpoint.description: TokenEndpoint is the URL for the OAuth token endpoint.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
OIDCConfig contains OIDC-specific configuration.
Required when Type is "oidc", must be nil when Type is "oauth2".

additional_authorization_params.description:
AdditionalAuthorizationParams are extra query parameters to include in
authorization requests. Useful for provider-specific parameters like
Google's access_type=offline.

client_id.description: ClientID is the OAuth 2.0 client identifier registered with the upstream IDP.

client_secret_env_var.description:
ClientSecretEnvVar is the name of an environment variable containing the client secret.
Mutually exclusive with ClientSecretFile. Optional for public clients using PKCE.

client_secret_file.description:
ClientSecretFile is the path to a file containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret.
Mutually exclusive with ClientSecretEnvVar. Optional for public clients using PKCE.

issuer_url.description:
IssuerURL is the OIDC issuer URL for automatic endpoint discovery.
Must be a valid HTTPS URL.

redirect_uri.description:
RedirectURI is the callback URL where the upstream IDP will redirect after authentication.
When not specified, defaults to `{issuer}/oauth/callback`.

scopes.description:
Scopes are the OAuth scopes to request from the upstream IDP.
If not specified, defaults to ["openid", "offline_access"].
When using AdditionalAuthorizationParams with provider-specific refresh
token mechanisms (e.g., Google's access_type=offline), set explicit scopes
to avoid sending both offline_access and the provider-specific parameter.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
EmbeddedAuthServerConfig contains configuration for the embedded OAuth2/OIDC authorization server.
When set, the proxy runner will start an embedded auth server that delegates to upstream IDPs.
This is the serializable RunConfig; secrets are referenced by file paths or env var names.

allowed_audiences.description:
AllowedAudiences is the list of valid resource URIs that tokens can be issued for.
Per RFC 8707, the "resource" parameter in authorization and token requests is
validated against this list. Required for MCP compliance.

authorization_endpoint_base_url.description:
AuthorizationEndpointBaseURL overrides the base URL used for the authorization_endpoint
in the OAuth discovery document. When set, the discovery document will advertise
`{authorization_endpoint_base_url}/oauth/authorize` instead of `{issuer}/oauth/authorize`.
All other endpoints remain derived from the issuer.

hmac_secret_files.description:
HMACSecretFiles contains file paths to HMAC secrets for signing authorization codes
and refresh tokens (opaque tokens).
First file is the current secret (must be at least 32 bytes), subsequent files
are for rotation/verification of existing tokens.
If empty, an ephemeral secret will be auto-generated (development only).

issuer.description:
Issuer is the issuer identifier for this authorization server.
This will be included in the "iss" claim of issued tokens.
Must be a valid HTTPS URL (or HTTP for localhost) without query, fragment, or trailing slash.

schema_version.description: SchemaVersion is the version of the RunConfig schema.

scopes_supported.description:
ScopesSupported lists the OAuth 2.0 scope values advertised in discovery documents.
If empty, defaults to registration.DefaultScopes (["openid", "profile", "email", "offline_access"]).

upstreams.description:
Upstreams configures connections to upstream Identity Providers.
At least one upstream is required - the server delegates authentication to these providers.
Multiple upstreams are supported for sequential authorization chains.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
SigningKeyConfig configures the signing key provider for JWT operations.
If nil or empty, an ephemeral signing key will be auto-generated (development only).

fallback_key_files.description:
FallbackKeyFiles are filenames of additional keys for verification (relative to KeyDir).
These keys are included in the JWKS endpoint for token verification but are NOT
used for signing new tokens. Useful for key rotation.

key_dir.description:
KeyDir is the directory containing PEM-encoded private key files.
All key filenames are relative to this directory.
In Kubernetes, this is typically a mounted Secret volume.

signing_key_file.description:
SigningKeyFile is the filename of the primary signing key (relative to KeyDir).
This key is used for signing new tokens.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
TokenLifespans configures the duration that various tokens are valid.
If nil, defaults are applied (access: 1h, refresh: 7d, authCode: 10m).

access_token_lifespan.description:
AccessTokenLifespan is the duration that access tokens are valid.
If empty, defaults to 1 hour.

auth_code_lifespan.description:
AuthCodeLifespan is the duration that authorization codes are valid.
If empty, defaults to 10 minutes.

refresh_token_lifespan.description:
RefreshTokenLifespan is the duration that refresh tokens are valid.
If empty, defaults to 7 days (168h).

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
TokenResponseMapping configures custom field extraction from non-standard token responses.
When set, the token exchange bypasses golang.org/x/oauth2 and extracts fields using
the configured dot-notation paths.

access_token_path.description: AccessTokenPath is the dot-notation path to the access token (required).

expires_in_path.description: ExpiresInPath is the dot-notation path to the expires_in value. Defaults to "expires_in".

refresh_token_path.description: RefreshTokenPath is the dot-notation path to the refresh token. Defaults to "refresh_token".

scope_path.description: ScopePath is the dot-notation path to the scope. Defaults to "scope".

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description: Type specifies the provider type: "oidc" or "oauth2".

name.description:
Name uniquely identifies this upstream.
Used for routing decisions and session binding in multi-upstream scenarios.
If empty when only one upstream is configured, defaults to "default".

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
FieldMapping contains custom field mapping configuration for non-standard providers.
If nil, standard OIDC field names are used ("sub", "name", "email").

email_fields.description:
EmailFields is an ordered list of field names to try for the email address.
The first non-empty value found will be used.
Default: ["email"]

name_fields.description:
NameFields is an ordered list of field names to try for the display name.
The first non-empty value found will be used.
Default: ["name"]

subject_fields.description:
SubjectFields is an ordered list of field names to try for the user ID.
The first non-empty value found will be used.
Default: ["sub"]

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description: UserInfo contains configuration for fetching user information (required for OAuth2).

additional_headers.description:
AdditionalHeaders contains extra headers to include in the userinfo request.
Useful for providers that require specific headers (e.g., GitHub's Accept header).

endpoint_url.description: EndpointURL is the URL of the userinfo endpoint.

http_method.description:
HTTPMethod is the HTTP method to use for the userinfo request.
If not specified, defaults to GET.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description: ACLUserConfig contains ACL user authentication configuration.

password_env_var.description: PasswordEnvVar is the environment variable containing the Redis password.

username_env_var.description: UsernameEnvVar is the environment variable containing the Redis username.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description: RedisConfig is the Redis-specific configuration when Type is "redis".

auth_type.description: AuthType must be "aclUser" - only ACL user authentication is supported.

dial_timeout.description: DialTimeout is the timeout for establishing connections (e.g., "5s").

read_timeout.description: ReadTimeout is the timeout for read operations (e.g., "3s").

write_timeout.description: WriteTimeout is the timeout for write operations (e.g., "3s").

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
SentinelTLS configures TLS for Sentinel connections.
Falls back to TLS config when nil.

ca_cert_file.description: CACertFile is the path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate file.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authser.description:
Storage configures the storage backend for the auth server.
If nil, defaults to in-memory storage.

type.description: Type specifies the storage backend type. Defaults to "memory".

db.description: DB is the Redis database number (default: 0).

master_name.description: MasterName is the name of the Redis Sentinel master.

sentinel_addrs.description: SentinelAddrs is the list of Sentinel addresses (host:port).

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_authz.C.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
AuthzConfig contains the authorization configuration

type.description: Type is the type of authorization configuration (e.g., "cedarv1").

version.description: Version is the version of the configuration format.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_client..description: ClientType is the type of MCP client

installed.description: Installed indicates whether the client is installed on the system

registered.description: Registered indicates whether the client is registered in the ToolHive configuration

supports_skills.description: SupportsSkills indicates whether ToolHive can install skills for this client

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_contain.description:
RuntimeConfig allows overriding the default runtime configuration
for this specific workload (base images and packages)

additional_packages.description:
AdditionalPackages lists extra packages to install in the builder and
runtime stages.
Examples for Alpine: ["git", "make", "gcc"]
Examples for Debian: ["git", "build-essential"]

builder_image.description:
BuilderImage is the full image reference for the builder stage.
An empty string signals "use the default for this transport type" during config merging.
Examples: "golang:1.26-alpine", "node:24-alpine", "python:3.14-slim"

created_at.description: CreatedAt is the timestamp when the workload was created.

group.description: Group is the name of the group this workload belongs to, if any.

labels.description: Labels are the container labels (excluding standard ToolHive labels)

name.description:
Name is the name of the workload.
It is used as a unique identifier.

package.description: Package specifies the Workload Package used to create this Workload.

port.description:
Port is the port on which the workload is exposed.
This is embedded in the URL.

proxy_mode.description:
ProxyMode is the proxy mode that clients should use to connect.
For stdio transports, this will be the proxy mode (sse or streamable-http).
For direct transports (sse/streamable-http), this will be the same as TransportType.

remote.description: Remote indicates whether this is a remote workload (true) or a container workload (false).

started_at.description: StartedAt is when the container was last started (changes on restart)

status_context.description:
StatusContext provides additional context about the workload's status.
The exact meaning is determined by the status and the underlying runtime.

tools.description: ToolsFilter is the filter on tools applied to the workload.

url.description: URL is the URL of the workload exposed by the ToolHive proxy.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_ignore..description: IgnoreConfig contains configuration for ignore processing

loadGlobal.description: Whether to load global ignore patterns

printOverlays.description: Whether to print resolved overlay paths for debugging

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_registr.description:
AuthConfig contains the non-secret OAuth configuration when auth is configured.
Nil when auth_status is "none".

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_runner..description: HeaderForward contains configuration for injecting headers into requests to remote servers.

add_headers_from_secret.description:
AddHeadersFromSecret is a map of header names to secret names.
The key is the header name, the value is the secret name in ToolHive's secrets manager.
Resolved at runtime via WithSecrets() into resolvedHeaders.
The actual secret value is only held in memory, never persisted.

add_plaintext_headers.description:
AddPlaintextHeaders is a map of header names to literal values to inject into requests.
WARNING: These values are stored in plaintext in the configuration.
For sensitive values (API keys, tokens), use AddHeadersFromSecret instead.

allow_docker_gateway.description:
AllowDockerGateway permits outbound connections to Docker gateway addresses
(host.docker.internal, gateway.docker.internal, 172.17.0.1). These are
blocked by default in the egress proxy even when InsecureAllowAll is set.
Only applicable to Docker deployments with network isolation enabled.

audit_config_path.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
AuditConfigPath is the path to the audit configuration file

authz_config_path.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
AuthzConfigPath is the path to the authorization configuration file

base_name.description: BaseName is the base name used for the container (without prefixes)

cmd_args.description: CmdArgs are the arguments to pass to the container

container_labels.description: ContainerLabels are the labels to apply to the container

container_name.description: ContainerName is the name of the container

debug.description: Debug indicates whether debug mode is enabled

endpoint_prefix.description:
EndpointPrefix is an explicit prefix to prepend to SSE endpoint URLs.
This is used to handle path-based ingress routing scenarios.

env_file_dir.description:
DEPRECATED: No longer appears to be used.
EnvFileDir is the directory path to load environment files from

env_vars.description: EnvVars are the parsed environment variables as key-value pairs

group.description: Group is the name of the group this workload belongs to, if any

host.description: Host is the host for the HTTP proxy

image.description: Image is the Docker image to run

isolate_network.description: IsolateNetwork indicates whether to isolate the network for the container

jwks_auth_token_file.description:
DEPRECATED: No longer appears to be used.
JWKSAuthTokenFile is the path to file containing auth token for JWKS/OIDC requests

k8s_pod_template_patch.description:
K8sPodTemplatePatch is a JSON string to patch the Kubernetes pod template
Only applicable when using Kubernetes runtime

middleware_configs.description:
MiddlewareConfigs contains the list of middleware to apply to the transport
and the configuration for each middleware.

mutating_webhooks.description:
MutatingWebhooks contains the configuration for mutating webhook middleware.
Mutating webhooks run before validating webhooks, per RFC THV-0017 ordering.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCP server

permission_profile_name_or_path.description: PermissionProfileNameOrPath is the name or path of the permission profile

port.description: Port is the port for the HTTP proxy to listen on (host port)

publish.description: Publish lists ports to publish to the host in format "hostPort:containerPort"

rate_limit_namespace.description: RateLimitNamespace is the Kubernetes namespace for Redis key derivation.

registry_api_url.description:
RegistryAPIURL is the registry API URL that served this server's metadata.
Empty when the server was not discovered via registry lookup.

registry_server_name.description:
RegistryServerName is the registry entry name used to look up this server's metadata.
Empty when the server was not discovered via registry lookup.

registry_url.description:
RegistryURL is the registry URL that served this server's metadata.
Empty when the server was not discovered via registry lookup.

remote_url.description: RemoteURL is the URL of the remote MCP server (if running remotely)

schema_version.description: SchemaVersion is the version of the RunConfig schema

secrets.description:
Secrets are the secret parameters to pass to the container
Format: "<secret name>,target=<target environment variable>"

stateless.description:
Stateless indicates the server only supports POST (no SSE/GET).
When true, the proxy returns 405 for incoming GET requests and uses a
POST-based health check instead of the default GET probe.
Applies to both remote URLs and local container workloads.

target_host.description: TargetHost is the host to forward traffic to (only applicable to SSE transport)

target_port.description: TargetPort is the port for the container to expose (only applicable to SSE transport)

thv_ca_bundle.description:
DEPRECATED: No longer appears to be used.
ThvCABundle is the path to the CA certificate bundle for ToolHive HTTP operations

tools_filter.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
ToolsFilter is the list of tools to filter

tools_override.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
ToolsOverride is a map from an actual tool to its overridden name and/or description

trust_proxy_headers.description: TrustProxyHeaders indicates whether to trust X-Forwarded-* headers from reverse proxies

validating_webhooks.description: ValidatingWebhooks contains the configuration for validating webhook middleware.

volumes.description:
Volumes are the directory mounts to pass to the container
Format: "host-path:container-path[:ro]"

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_runner..description:
ScalingConfig contains configuration for horizontal scaling of the proxy runner.
Only applicable when running in Kubernetes with the ToolHive operator.
When nil, no scaling configuration is applied (single-replica default behavior).

backend_replicas.description:
BackendReplicas is the desired StatefulSet replica count for the proxy runner backend.
When nil, replicas are unmanaged (preserving HPA or manual kubectl control).
When set (including 0), the value is an explicit replica count.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_runner..description:
SessionRedis holds non-sensitive Redis connection parameters for distributed session storage.
Populated only when MCPServer.spec.sessionStorage.provider == "redis".
The Redis password is not included — it is injected as env var THV_SESSION_REDIS_PASSWORD.
+optional

address.description: Address is the Redis server address (host:port).

db.description: DB is the Redis database number.

key_prefix.description: KeyPrefix is an optional prefix applied to all Redis keys used by ToolHive.

description.description: Description is the redefined description of the tool

name.description: Name is the redefined name of the tool

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_secrets.description: Bearer token for authentication (alternative to OAuth)

reference.description: Reference is the OCI reference of the built skill artifact.

digest.description: Digest is the OCI digest for upgrade detection.

name.description: Name is the dependency name.

reference.description: Reference is the OCI reference for the dependency.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_skills..description: Status is the current installation status.
InstalledSkill contains the full installation record.

clients.description:
Clients is the list of client identifiers the skill is installed for.
TODO: Refactor client.ClientApp to a shared package so it can be used here instead of []string.

dependencies.description: Dependencies is the list of external skill dependencies.

digest.description: Digest is the OCI digest (sha256:...) for upgrade detection.

installed_at.description: InstalledAt is the timestamp when the skill was installed.

project_root.description: ProjectRoot is the project root path for project-scoped skills. Empty for user-scoped.

reference.description: Reference is the full OCI reference (e.g. ghcr.io/org/skill:v1).

tag.description: Tag is the OCI tag (e.g. v1.0.0).

description.description: Description is the skill description extracted from the artifact metadata, if available.

digest.description: Digest is the OCI digest of the artifact (sha256:...).

name.description: Name is the skill name extracted from the artifact metadata, if available.

tag.description: Tag is the OCI tag or name used to reference the artifact.

version.description: Version is the skill version extracted from the artifact metadata, if available.

body.description: Body is the raw SKILL.md markdown content.

description.description: Description is the skill description from the OCI config labels.

files.description: Files is the list of all files in the artifact with their sizes.

license.description: License is the SPDX license identifier from the OCI config labels.

name.description: Name is the skill name from the OCI config labels.

version.description: Version is the skill version from the OCI config labels.

path.description: Path is the file path within the artifact.

size.description: Size is the uncompressed file size in bytes.

author.description: Author is the skill author or maintainer.

description.description: Description is a human-readable description of the skill.

name.description: Name is the unique name of the skill.

tags.description: Tags is a list of tags for categorization.

version.description: Version is the semantic version of the skill.

errors.description: Errors is a list of validation errors, if any.

valid.description: Valid indicates whether the skill definition is valid.

warnings.description: Warnings is a list of non-blocking validation warnings, if any.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_telemet.description:
DEPRECATED: Middleware configuration.
TelemetryConfig contains the OpenTelemetry configuration

caCertPath.description:
CACertPath is the file path to a CA certificate bundle for the OTLP endpoint.
When set, the OTLP exporters use this CA to verify the collector's TLS certificate
instead of relying solely on the system CA pool.
+optional

customAttributes.description:
CustomAttributes contains custom resource attributes to be added to all telemetry signals.
These are parsed from CLI flags (--otel-custom-attributes) or environment variables
(OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES) as key=value pairs.
+optional

enablePrometheusMetricsPath.description:
EnablePrometheusMetricsPath controls whether to expose Prometheus-style /metrics endpoint.
The metrics are served on the main transport port at /metrics.
This is separate from OTLP metrics which are sent to the Endpoint.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

endpoint.description:
Endpoint is the OTLP endpoint URL
+optional

environmentVariables.description:
EnvironmentVariables is a list of environment variable names that should be
included in telemetry spans as attributes. Only variables in this list will
be read from the host machine and included in spans for observability.
Example: ["NODE_ENV", "DEPLOYMENT_ENV", "SERVICE_VERSION"]
+optional

headers.description:
Headers contains authentication headers for the OTLP endpoint.
+optional

insecure.description:
Insecure indicates whether to use HTTP instead of HTTPS for the OTLP endpoint.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

metricsEnabled.description:
MetricsEnabled controls whether OTLP metrics are enabled.
When false, OTLP metrics are not sent even if an endpoint is configured.
This is independent of EnablePrometheusMetricsPath.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

samplingRate.description:
SamplingRate is the trace sampling rate (0.0-1.0) as a string.
Only used when TracingEnabled is true.
Example: "0.05" for 5% sampling.
+kubebuilder:default="0.05"
+optional

serviceName.description:
ServiceName is the service name for telemetry.
When omitted, defaults to the server name (e.g., VirtualMCPServer name).
+optional

serviceVersion.description:
ServiceVersion is the service version for telemetry.
When omitted, defaults to the ToolHive version.
+optional

tracingEnabled.description:
TracingEnabled controls whether distributed tracing is enabled.
When false, no tracer provider is created even if an endpoint is configured.
+kubebuilder:default=false
+optional

useLegacyAttributes.description:
UseLegacyAttributes controls whether legacy (pre-MCP OTEL semconv) attribute names
are emitted alongside the new standard attribute names. When true, spans include both
old and new attribute names for backward compatibility with existing dashboards.
Currently defaults to true; this will change to false in a future release.
+kubebuilder:default=true
+optional

parameters.description:
Parameters is a JSON object containing the middleware parameters.
It is stored as a raw message to allow flexible parameter types.

type.description: Type is a string representing the middleware type.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_transpo.description:
ProxyMode is the effective HTTP protocol the proxy uses.
For stdio transports, this is the configured mode (sse or streamable-http).
For direct transports (sse/streamable-http), this matches the transport type.
Note: "sse" is deprecated; use "streamable-http" instead.
Transport is the transport mode (stdio, sse, or streamable-http)

hmac_secret_ref.description: HMACSecretRef is an optional reference to an HMAC secret for payload signing.

name.description: Name is a unique identifier for this webhook.

timeout.description: Timeout is the maximum time to wait for a webhook response.

url.description: URL is the HTTPS endpoint to call.

github_com_stacklok_toolhive_pkg_webhook.description: FailurePolicy determines behavior when the webhook call fails.
TLSConfig holds optional TLS configuration (CA bundles, client certs).

ca_bundle_path.description: CABundlePath is the path to a CA certificate bundle for server verification.

client_cert_path.description: ClientCertPath is the path to a client certificate for mTLS.

client_key_path.description: ClientKeyPath is the path to a client key for mTLS.

insecure_skip_verify.description:
InsecureSkipVerify disables server certificate verification.
WARNING: This should only be used for development/testing.

environmentVariables.description: EnvironmentVariables are set when running the package

fileSha256.description: FileSHA256 is the SHA-256 hash for integrity verification (required for mcpb, optional for others)

identifier.description:
Identifier is the package identifier:
  - For NPM/PyPI/NuGet: package name or ID
  - For OCI: full image reference (e.g., "ghcr.io/owner/repo:v1.0.0")
  - For MCPB: direct download URL

packageArguments.description: PackageArguments are passed to the package's binary

registryBaseUrl.description: RegistryBaseURL is the base URL of the package registry (used by npm, pypi, nuget; not used by oci, mcpb)

registryType.description: RegistryType indicates how to download packages (e.g., "npm", "pypi", "oci", "nuget", "mcpb")

runtimeArguments.description: RuntimeArguments are passed to the package's runtime command (e.g., docker, npx)

runtimeHint.description: RunTimeHint suggests the appropriate runtime for the package

version.description: Version is the package version (required for npm, pypi, nuget; optional for mcpb; not used by oci where version is in the identifier)

model.Transport.description: Transport is required and specifies the transport protocol configuration

allow_host.description: AllowHost is a list of allowed hosts for inbound connections

mode.description:
Mode specifies the network mode for the container (e.g., "host", "bridge", "none")
When empty, the default container runtime network mode is used

allow_host.description: AllowHost is a list of allowed hosts

allow_port.description: AllowPort is a list of allowed ports

insecure_allow_all.description: InsecureAllowAll allows all outbound network connections

name.description: Name is the name of the profile

privileged.description:
Privileged indicates whether the container should run in privileged mode
When true, the container has access to all host devices and capabilities
Use with extreme caution as this removes most security isolation

read.description:
Read is a list of mount declarations that the container can read from
These can be in the following formats:
- A single path: The same path will be mounted from host to container
- host-path:container-path: Different paths for host and container
- resource-uri:container-path: Mount a resource identified by URI to a container path

write.description:
Write is a list of mount declarations that the container can write to
These follow the same format as Read mounts but with write permissions

pkg_api_v1.RegistryType.description: Type of registry (file, url, or default)

allow_private_ip.description: Allow private IP addresses for registry URL or API URL

pkg_api_v1.buildListResponse.description: Response containing a list of locally-built OCI skill artifacts

builds.description: List of locally-built OCI skill artifacts

pkg_api_v1.buildSkillRequest.description: Request to build a skill from a local directory

path.description: Path to the skill definition directory

tag.description: OCI tag for the built artifact

groups.description: Groups is the list of groups configured on the client.

names.description: Names is the list of client names to operate on.

group.description: Group name to operate on (mutually exclusive with names)

names.description: Names of the workloads to operate on

groups.description: Groups is the list of groups configured on the client.
Groups is the list of groups configured on the client.

name.description: Name of the group to create
Name of the created group

pkg_api_v1.createRequest.description: Request to create a new workload

cmd_arguments.description: Command arguments to pass to the container

env_vars.description: Environment variables to set in the container

group.description: Group name this workload belongs to

name.description: Name of the workload

network_isolation.description: Whether network isolation is turned on. This applies the rules in the permission profile.

proxy_port.description: Port for the HTTP proxy to listen on

registry.description: Registry is the optional registry name to resolve the server from (e.g. "default").

server.description:
Server is the optional server name in the registry (e.g. "io.github.stacklok/fetch").
When both Registry and Server are set, thv resolves the server metadata
server-side, filling in image, transport, env vars, permissions, etc.
User-provided fields always override registry defaults.

target_port.description: Port to expose from the container

trust_proxy_headers.description: Whether to trust X-Forwarded-* headers from reverse proxies

pkg_api_v1.createSecretRequest.description: Request to create a new secret

pkg_api_v1.createWorkloadResponse.description: Response after successfully creating a workload

name.description: Name of the created workload

port.description: Port the workload is listening on

auth_status.description:
AuthStatus is one of: "none", "configured", "authenticated".
Intentionally omits omitempty — see registryInfo for rationale.

auth_type.description:
AuthType is "oauth", "bearer" (future), or empty string when no auth.
Intentionally omits omitempty — see registryInfo for rationale.

name.description: Name of the registry

server_count.description: Number of servers in the registry

source.description: Source of the registry (URL, file path, or empty string for built-in)

name.description: Name of the secrets provider

is_remote.description: Indicates if this is a remote server

pkg_api_v1.headerForwardConfig.description:
HeaderForward configures headers to inject into requests to remote MCP servers.
Use this to add custom headers like X-Tenant-ID or correlation IDs.

add_headers_from_secret.description:
AddHeadersFromSecret maps header names to secret names in ToolHive's secrets manager.
Key: HTTP header name, Value: secret name in the secrets manager

add_plaintext_headers.description:
AddPlaintextHeaders contains literal header values to inject.
WARNING: These values are stored and transmitted in plaintext.
Use AddHeadersFromSecret for sensitive data like API keys.

clients.description:
Clients lists target client identifiers (e.g., "claude-code"),
or ["all"] to target every skill-supporting client.
Omitting this field installs to all available clients.

force.description: Force allows overwriting unmanaged skill directories

group.description: Group is the group name to add the skill to after installation

name.description: Name or OCI reference of the skill to install

project_root.description: ProjectRoot is the project root path for project-scoped installs

pkg_api_v1.installSkillResponse.description: Response after successfully installing a skill

pkg_api_v1.listSecretsResponse.description: Response containing a list of secret keys

pkg_api_v1.listServersResponse.description: Response containing a list of servers

remote_servers.description: List of remote servers in the registry (if any)

servers.description: List of container servers in the registry

scopes.description: OAuth scopes to advertise in well-known endpoint (RFC 9728)

limit.description: Limit is the maximum number of items per page

page.description: Page is the current page number (1-based)

total.description: Total is the total number of items matching the query

can_cleanup.description: Whether the provider can cleanup all secrets

can_delete.description: Whether the provider can delete secrets

can_list.description: Whether the provider can list secrets

can_read.description: Whether the provider can read secrets

can_write.description: Whether the provider can write secrets

pkg_api_v1.pushSkillRequest.description: Request to push a built skill artifact

pkg_api_v1.registryErrorResponse.description: Structured error response returned by registry endpoints

code.description: Code is a machine-readable error code (e.g. "not_found", "registry_auth_required")

message.description: Message is a human-readable description of the error

auth_status.description:
AuthStatus is one of: "none", "configured", "authenticated".
Intentionally omits omitempty so clients always receive the field,
even when the value is "none" (the zero-value equivalent).

auth_type.description:
AuthType is "oauth", "bearer" (future), or empty string when no auth.
Intentionally omits omitempty so clients can distinguish "no auth
configured" (empty string) from "field missing" without extra logic.

name.description: Name of the registry

server_count.description: Number of servers in the registry

source.description: Source of the registry (URL, file path, or empty string for built-in)

pkg_api_v1.registryListResponse.description: Response containing a list of registries

pkg_api_v1.remoteOAuthConfig.description: OAuth configuration for remote server authentication

authorize_url.description: OAuth authorization endpoint URL (alternative to issuer for non-OIDC OAuth)

callback_port.description: Specific port for OAuth callback server

oauth_params.description: Additional OAuth parameters for server-specific customization

skip_browser.description: Whether to skip opening browser for OAuth flow (defaults to false)

token_url.description: OAuth token endpoint URL (alternative to issuer for non-OIDC OAuth)

use_pkce.description: Whether to use PKCE for the OAuth flow

pkg_api_v1.serversV01Response.description: Paginated list of servers from the registry

servers.description: Servers is the list of servers on the current page

pkg_api_v1.setupSecretsRequest.description: Request to setup a secrets provider

password.description:
Password for encrypted provider (optional, can be set via environment variable)
TODO Review environment variable for this

provider_type.description: Type of the secrets provider (encrypted, 1password, environment)

pkg_api_v1.setupSecretsResponse.description: Response after initializing a secrets provider

provider_type.description: Type of the secrets provider that was setup

pkg_api_v1.skillListResponse.description: Response containing a list of installed skills

pkg_api_v1.skillsV01Response.description: Paginated list of skills from the registry

skills.description: Skills is the list of skills on the current page

name.description: Name of the tool

pkg_api_v1.updateRequest.description: Request to update an existing workload (name cannot be changed)

cmd_arguments.description: Command arguments to pass to the container

env_vars.description: Environment variables to set in the container

group.description: Group name this workload belongs to

network_isolation.description: Whether network isolation is turned on. This applies the rules in the permission profile.

proxy_port.description: Port for the HTTP proxy to listen on

target_port.description: Port to expose from the container

trust_proxy_headers.description: Whether to trust X-Forwarded-* headers from reverse proxies

pkg_api_v1.updateSecretRequest.description: Request to update an existing secret

path.description: Path to the skill definition directory

pkg_api_v1.workloadListResponse.description: Response containing a list of workloads

workloads.description: List of container information for each workload

default.description:
Default is the value to use if the environment variable is not explicitly provided
Only used for non-required variables

description.description: Description is a human-readable explanation of the variable's purpose

name.description: Name is the environment variable name (e.g., API_KEY)

required.description:
Required indicates whether this environment variable must be provided
If true and not provided via command line or secrets, the user will be prompted for a value

secret.description:
Secret indicates whether this environment variable contains sensitive information
If true, the value will be stored as a secret rather than as a plain environment variable

description.description: Description is a human-readable description of the group's purpose and functionality

name.description: Name is the identifier for the group, used when referencing the group in commands

remote_servers.description: RemoteServers is a map of server names to their corresponding remote server definitions within this group

servers.description: Servers is a map of server names to their corresponding server definitions within this group

choices.description: Choices provides a list of valid values for the header (optional)

default.description:
Default is the value to use if the header is not explicitly provided
Only used for non-required headers

description.description: Description is a human-readable explanation of the header's purpose

name.description: Name is the header name (e.g., X-API-Key, Authorization)

required.description:
Required indicates whether this header must be provided
If true and not provided via command line or secrets, the user will be prompted for a value

secret.description:
Secret indicates whether this header contains sensitive information
If true, the value will be stored as a secret rather than as plain text

registry.ImageMetadata.description: Container server details (if it's a container server)

args.description:
Args are the default command-line arguments to pass to the MCP server container.
These arguments will be used only if no command-line arguments are provided by the user.
If the user provides arguments, they will override these defaults.

custom_metadata.description: CustomMetadata allows for additional user-defined metadata

description.description: Description is a human-readable description of the server's purpose and functionality

docker_tags.description: DockerTags lists the available Docker tags for this server image

env_vars.description: EnvVars defines environment variables that can be passed to the server

image.description: Image is the Docker image reference for the MCP server

name.description:
Name is the identifier for the MCP server, used when referencing the server in commands
If not provided, it will be auto-generated from the registry key

overview.description:
Overview is a longer Markdown-formatted description for web display.
Unlike the Description field (limited to 500 chars), this supports
full Markdown and is intended for rich rendering on catalog pages.

proxy_port.description:
ProxyPort is the port for the HTTP proxy to listen on (host port)
If not specified, a random available port will be assigned

repository_url.description: RepositoryURL is the URL to the source code repository for the server

status.description: Status indicates whether the server is currently active or deprecated

tags.description: Tags are categorization labels for the server to aid in discovery and filtering

target_port.description: TargetPort is the port for the container to expose (only applicable to SSE and Streamable HTTP transports)

tier.description: Tier represents the tier classification level of the server, e.g., "Official" or "Community"

title.description:
Title is an optional human-readable display name for the server.
If not provided, the Name field is used for display purposes.

tools.description: Tools is a list of tool names provided by this MCP server

transport.description:
Transport defines the communication protocol for the server
For containers: stdio, sse, or streamable-http
For remote servers: sse or streamable-http (stdio not supported)

registry.KubernetesMetadata.description:
Kubernetes contains Kubernetes-specific metadata when the MCP server is deployed in a cluster.
This field is optional and only populated when:
- The server is served from ToolHive Registry Server
- The server was auto-discovered from a Kubernetes deployment
- The Kubernetes resource has the required registry annotations

image.description: Image is the container image used by the Kubernetes workload (applicable to MCPServer)

kind.description: Kind is the Kubernetes resource kind (e.g., MCPServer, VirtualMCPServer, MCPRemoteProxy)

name.description: Name is the Kubernetes resource name

namespace.description: Namespace is the Kubernetes namespace where the resource is deployed

transport.description: Transport is the transport type configured for the Kubernetes workload (applicable to MCPServer)

uid.description: UID is the Kubernetes resource UID

registry.Metadata.description: Metadata contains additional information about the server such as popularity metrics

last_updated.description: LastUpdated is the timestamp when the server was last updated, in RFC3339 format

stars.description: Stars represents the popularity rating or number of stars for the server

registry.OAuthConfig.description:
OAuthConfig provides OAuth/OIDC configuration for authentication to the remote server
Used with the thv proxy command's --remote-auth flags

authorize_url.description:
AuthorizeURL is the OAuth authorization endpoint URL
Used for non-OIDC OAuth flows when issuer is not provided

callback_port.description:
CallbackPort is the specific port to use for the OAuth callback server
If not specified, a random available port will be used

client_id.description: ClientID is the OAuth client ID for authentication

issuer.description:
Issuer is the OAuth/OIDC issuer URL (e.g., https://accounts.google.com)
Used for OIDC discovery to find authorization and token endpoints

oauth_params.description:
OAuthParams contains additional OAuth parameters to include in the authorization request
These are server-specific parameters like "prompt", "response_mode", etc.

resource.description: Resource is the OAuth 2.0 resource indicator (RFC 8707)

scopes.description:
Scopes are the OAuth scopes to request
If not specified, defaults to ["openid", "profile", "email"] for OIDC

token_url.description:
TokenURL is the OAuth token endpoint URL
Used for non-OIDC OAuth flows when issuer is not provided

use_pkce.description:
UsePKCE indicates whether to use PKCE for the OAuth flow
Defaults to true for enhanced security

registry.Provenance.description: Provenance contains verification and signing metadata

registry.RemoteServerMetadata.description: Remote server details (if it's a remote server)

custom_metadata.description: CustomMetadata allows for additional user-defined metadata

description.description: Description is a human-readable description of the server's purpose and functionality

env_vars.description:
EnvVars defines environment variables that can be passed to configure the client
These might be needed for client-side configuration when connecting to the remote server

headers.description:
Headers defines HTTP headers that can be passed to the remote server for authentication
These are used with the thv proxy command's authentication features

name.description:
Name is the identifier for the MCP server, used when referencing the server in commands
If not provided, it will be auto-generated from the registry key

overview.description:
Overview is a longer Markdown-formatted description for web display.
Unlike the Description field (limited to 500 chars), this supports
full Markdown and is intended for rich rendering on catalog pages.

proxy_port.description:
ProxyPort is the port for the HTTP proxy to listen on (host port)
If not specified, a random available port will be assigned

repository_url.description: RepositoryURL is the URL to the source code repository for the server

status.description: Status indicates whether the server is currently active or deprecated

tags.description: Tags are categorization labels for the server to aid in discovery and filtering

tier.description: Tier represents the tier classification level of the server, e.g., "Official" or "Community"

title.description:
Title is an optional human-readable display name for the server.
If not provided, the Name field is used for display purposes.

tools.description: Tools is a list of tool names provided by this MCP server

transport.description:
Transport defines the communication protocol for the server
For containers: stdio, sse, or streamable-http
For remote servers: sse or streamable-http (stdio not supported)

url.description: URL is the endpoint URL for the remote MCP server (e.g., https://api.example.com/mcp)

_meta.description: Meta is an opaque payload with extended meta data details of the skill.

allowedTools.description:
AllowedTools is the list of tools that the skill is compatible with.
This is experimental.

compatibility.description: Compatibility is the environment requirements of the skill.

description.description: Description is the description of the skill.

icons.description: Icons is the list of icons for the skill.

license.description: License is the SPDX license identifier of the skill.

metadata.description:
Metadata is the official metadata of the skill as reported in the
SKILL.md file.

name.description:
Name is the name of the skill.
The format is that of identifiers, e.g. "my-skill".

namespace.description:
Namespace is the namespace of the skill.
The format is reverse-DNS, e.g. "io.github.user".

packages.description: Packages is the list of packages for the skill.

status.description:
Status is the status of the skill.
Can be one of "active", "deprecated", or "archived".

title.description:
Title is the title of the skill.
This is for human consumption, not an identifier.

version.description:
Version is the version of the skill.
Any non-empty string is valid, but ideally it should be either a
semantic version or a commit hash.

label.description: Label is the label of the icon.

size.description: Size is the size of the icon.

src.description: Src is the source of the icon.

type.description: Type is the type of the icon.

commit.description: Commit is the commit of the package.

digest.description: Digest is the digest of the package.

identifier.description: Identifier is the OCI identifier of the package.

mediaType.description: MediaType is the media type of the package.

ref.description: Ref is the reference of the package.

registryType.description:
RegistryType is the type of registry the package is from.
Can be "oci" or "git".

subfolder.description: Subfolder is the subfolder of the package.

url.description: URL is the URL of the package.

registry.SkillRepository.description: Repository is the source repository of the skill.

type.description: Type is the type of the repository.

url.description: URL is the URL of the repository.

description.example: MCP server providing weather data and forecasts via OpenWeatherMap API

v1.Duration.description:
RefillPeriod is the duration to fully refill the bucket from zero to maxTokens.
The effective refill rate is maxTokens / refillPeriod tokens per second.
Format: Go duration string (e.g., "1m0s", "30s", "1h0m0s").
+kubebuilder:validation:Required
